summary: Completed read-only R7 consensus review for install/update/new-PC/GitHub recovery equivalence. Inspected the revised unfinished-improvement document and installer/release verifier evidence. Verdict: AGREE because the document accurately treats install/release equivalence as unfinished work with required hard gates rather than claiming current completion.
decisions: The reviewed document is adequate as a completion plan for install/update/new-PC equivalence because it requires app/data ref parity, guided hook trust, build/index refresh, launcher/uninstaller proof, ZIP/SHA/tag/asset parity, version drift detection, and verify:goal hard-gating.
The document correctly identifies missing or unmapped verifiers such as verify:install-equivalence and verify:release-manifest, so it does not false-claim current completion.
next_steps: Implement or map verify:install-equivalence and verify:release-manifest into verify:goal before any final readiness claim.
Add durable release manifest proof with app commit, data commit, installer/package version, ZIP path/SHA, tag target, asset evidence when uploaded, and verifier statuses.
